danger_signals.visual_form: Dr. Fred, Nurse Edna, Weird Ed character sprites
danger_signals.meaning: NPCs will attack on sight; indicate threat zones and dungeon capture risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Two disembodied tentacles (purple and green) present in mansion
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental manifestation of mind-control threat; sentient meteor's control over Edison family creates unpredictability

danger_signals.visual_form: Lower mansion area accessible via specific rooms
danger_signals.meaning: Death or capture consequence zone; spatial danger marker

safety_signals.visual_form: Green tentacle NPC sprite (distinct from purple)
safety_signals.meaning: Only non-hostile mansion inhabitant; safe to interact with; ally signal

safety_signals.visual_form: Six playable characters with distinct visual designs (Bernard, Jeff, Michael, Razor, Syd, Wendy)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and alternative approach pathways; multiple solutions reduce single-point failure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dr. Fred (scientist), Nurse Edna (wife), Weird Ed (son) — all hostile
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion interior; scientific equipment; dungeon location; presence of two tentacles (purple control entity, green neutral)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dave Miller + two selected companions from friend group
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outside mansion initially; infiltration-based positioning; character-specific equipment/abilities visible

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Disembodied tentacle (purple variant); meteor origin 20 years prior
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Indirect threat through enslaved Edisons; no direct combat representation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mansion as affected space; brainwashing mechanic as world-state manifestation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Intro cutscene shows rescue mission necessity; Sandy absent from mansion until endgame
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs hostile on sight; dungeon holds kidnapped victim; puzzle solutions required to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two tentacles present in mansion; Edison family hostile sprites; irrational NPC behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies will capture/kill intruders; sentient meteor prevents normal family cooperation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Selected character removed from on-screen sprite group; party size visually reduced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Available puzzle solutions change; ability pool shrinks; game-over if all three party members eliminated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Room access and item availability changes based on which six characters selected
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Non-linear narrative paths; five possible endings based on character composition and survival

environmental_storytelling.example: All Edison family members except green tentacle actively hunt intruders; visual pursuit and dungeon capture mechanic communicates meteor-induced threat without explicit exposition
Syd/Razor carry musical instruments (visible in sprites); Bernard carries repair tools; visual equipment signals puzzle-solving capability to player
Purple tentacle (enslaving force) vs. green tentacle (ally) — color differentiation communicates alignment and control state without dialogue
Dungeon location as consequence zone; upper mansion as exploration space; architecture communicates risk escalation through depth/access
Intro cutscene establishes meteor origin (20 years prior), brainwashing context, and Sandy kidnapping — visual narrative framing of world-state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile on sight; captures intruders; prioritizes brain collection for meteor experiments
Hostile on sight; participates in capture/kill mechanics
Hostile on sight; mansion inhabitant threat
Non-hostile; allows interaction; safe to approach; visual differentiation from purple tentacle indicates independent will
Stealth/avoidance required; puzzle-solving for dungeon escape; death removes character from active group; ability-dependent task execution

notes: Maniac Mansion NES heavily reworked by Nintendo of America due to content concerns. Core visual manifestation relies on: (1) NPC hostility signaling danger through sprite animation and pursuit behavior; (2) tentacle duality (purple=control, green=ally) for faction alignment; (3) character visual differentiation for ability signaling; (4) spatial dungeon as consequence zone; (5) cutscene exposition for world-state context. The game communicates non-linearity through character selection affecting visual puzzle pathways and NPC interactions. No explicit UI danger indicators documented; threat communication is primarily behavioral (pursuit, capture) and spatial (dungeon location).
